name: Output Formatting
description: Output formatting module providing ANSI terminal colors, JSON serialization, clipboard integration, and markdown table rendering for the Ascend CLI tool

types.name: Any
types.description: Python typing.Any for arbitrary data types

functions.name: _colorize
functions.description: Apply ANSI color codes to markdown-formatted report text based on line patterns (headers, status keywords)
postconditions: Returns text with ANSI color codes applied
Headers (# and ##) are bold cyan
Subheaders (###) are bold
Lines containing 'Blocked' are red
Lines containing 'Active' or 'On Track' are green
Lines containing 'At Risk' are yellow
Lines starting with 'Generated:' or '---' are dim
Other lines remain unchanged

functions.name: print_report
functions.description: Print report text to stdout with optional ANSI coloring, auto-detecting TTY if use_color is not specified

inputs.name: use_color
inputs.description: Whether to apply ANSI colors; None auto-detects TTY

postconditions: Text is printed to stdout
If use_color is None, colors are applied only if stdout is a TTY
If use_color is True, colors are always applied
If use_color is False, no colors are applied

functions.name: print_json
functions.description: Print data as indented JSON to stdout using default=str for non-serializable objects

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Data to serialize and print as JSON

postconditions: Data is serialized to JSON with 2-space indentation
Non-serializable objects are converted to strings
JSON is printed to stdout

functions.name: print_status
functions.description: Print a status message to stderr with dim styling if stderr is a TTY, plain text otherwise
postconditions: Message is printed to stderr
If stderr is a TTY, message is dimmed with ANSI codes
If stderr is not a TTY, message is printed plain

functions.name: copy_to_clipboard
functions.description: Copy text to clipboard via pbcopy command (macOS-specific), with 5-second timeout

error_cases.name: timeout
error_cases.condition: pbcopy does not complete within 5 seconds

postconditions: Returns True if pbcopy command succeeds (returncode == 0)
Returns False if pbcopy times out, is not found, or raises OSError

structured_side_effects.description: Executes pbcopy command to copy text to system clipboard

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is text length

functions.name: format_table
functions.description: Format headers and rows as a markdown table with auto-calculated column widths

inputs.name: rows
inputs.description: Table rows, each row is a list of cell values

postconditions: Returns 'No data.' if rows is empty
Returns markdown table string if rows is non-empty
Column widths are calculated to fit headers and all cells
Table uses markdown pipe syntax with separators
Cells are left-justified and padded to column width
Missing cells in short rows are rendered as empty strings

performance_budget.complexity: O(rows * cols) where rows is number of rows and cols is number of columns

functions.name: render_output
functions.description: Unified output handler that prints data as JSON or markdown, optionally copying to clipboard

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Data to render (str for markdown, any for JSON)

inputs.name: json_mode
inputs.description: If True, always print as JSON

inputs.name: copy
inputs.description: If True, attempt to copy output to clipboard

postconditions: If json_mode is True, data is printed as JSON to stdout
If json_mode is False and data is str, prints as colored report
If json_mode is False and data is not str, prints as JSON to stdout
If copy is True, attempts to copy output to clipboard via copy_to_clipboard
side_effects: May copy to clipboard if copy=True

structured_side_effects.description: Conditionally copies to clipboard if copy=True

invariants: pbcopy timeout is fixed at 5 seconds
JSON output always uses 2-space indentation and default=str for serialization

data_access.rationale: Output module handles terminal I/O and clipboard interaction

side_effects.rationale: Multiple functions print to stdout for primary output
print_status writes status messages to stderr
copy_to_clipboard executes pbcopy subprocess to access system clipboard
